                                                                        delivery time between the Visa Application Center and Embassy/Consulate. Visa application submitted in Shanghai, Nanjing, Hangzhou Visa Application Center will be delivered to Consulate

                                                                        of your application being incomplete or delayed.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Individuals intending to visit family and friends legally residing in Sweden can apply for Schengen visa. An applicant will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Individuals intending to visit family and friends legally residing in Sweden can apply for Schengen visa. An applicant will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category.

## Visa Fees
Visa Fees applicable for a Schengen visa are listed in the table below.

[Table:Tourism]

## Service Fees
Service Charge for Visa Applications: 18 EUR / 145 CNY per person (inclusive of Service Taxes). It is subject to change every month.

## Documents Required
- Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/chn/en/swe/travel-insurance)
In general the applicant is advised to provide  documentation in original. Additional documentation may be required. In case  the applicant cannot give the VAC the original version of the document, a copy  must be added and the original shown at the VAC. Without special instruction,  an English translation must be attached to any documents in Chinese. 
Checklist
Visiting Family and Friends (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4tiAiYjuCmf8e3qaMERifw/ca049a939a2b87fb6c446c866ac883b8/visiting-family-and-friends.pdf)
Invitation form (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3033z0AJ6nXrQl8eHLEaAn/e15ccadab94b3306f0c03ed5e063957c/invitation-form-for-schengen-visa.pdf)
To help us process your application quickly and accurately, please complete the Sweden Visa Application Form (VAF) electronically using the editable PDF available on this site, print the typed copy and carry it with appointment letter during your visit to the visa application centre. Completing the form electronically reduces counter waiting time and helps avoid errors caused by illegible handwriting.
Note: Applicants who arrive without a form completed electronically will still be accepted with a completed handwritten form; however, they should expect additional processing time at Visa Application Centre.

## Photo Specifications
A Photograph submitted with the application form should comply with the following specifications:

- Latest photograph not older than 6 months
- Colour photograph printed on normal photographic paper
- Should be passport sized 45mm x 35mm
- 70-90% of the photograph should be covered by face
- Full face straight profile photograph, 30-36 mm from point of the chin to head
- Should be taken against a light background
- Should be without sunglasses, hat or cap
- Photograph can be taken with headgear, after consideration on case to case basis if the applicant wears headgear on religious grounds.
- Spectacles should not have toned or reflecting glasses. The frame of the spectacles may not cover the eyes
- The mouth must be closed
Please note that photographs should meet the above mentioned specifications

## Processing Time
The processing time is counted from the date when the Embassy of Sweden in Beijing/Consulate General of Sweden in Shanghai receives the application documents; it will not include the
                                                                        delivery time between the Visa Application Center and Embassy/Consulate. Visa application submitted in Shanghai, Nanjing, Hangzhou Visa Application Center will be delivered to Consulate
                                                                        General of Sweden in Shanghai for processing. Visa application submitted in other Visa Application Centers will be delivered to the Embassy of Sweden in Beijing for processing. 
Usual application processing time is 15 calendar days from the date when the Embassy of Sweden in Beijing/Consulate General of Sweden in Shanghai receives the application documents, the
                                                                        processing time could up to 45 days in some cases. A visa application should be submitted at least 15 calendar days (excluding the day of documents' submission) before the intended
                                                                        visit and cannot be submitted earlier than six months before the intended date of arrival in the Schengen Area.
Please note that you may be required to appear before a Visa Officer for a personal interview at the Embassy/ Consulate General of Sweden in China prior to a decision being taken in your
                                                                        case. Embassy/Consulate General of Sweden also reserves the right to ask for additional supporting documents during the visa application process. Applicants are requested to read
                                                                        the information on this site carefully. The guidelines listed on this site helps you to prepare your documents as accurately as possible as possible which will reduce the chances
                                                                        of your application being incomplete or delayed.

## Download Forms
Forms listed below must be filled in completely (in English or in Swedish languages only) and must be signed by the applicant himself/herself.
New visa application form can be downloaded and printed from the link below. Forms are also available at the visa application centre (free of charge)
Visa Application Form (English) (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/7l3Z43SaEWMBZ7h5sWZVan/6a1d9591f6615071d7c0ff7c3b1bc571/application-for-schengen-visa.pdf)
Visa  Application Form (Swedish) (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3aaTmmq0lcGbMtQZFuCOUW/906c276550993fb59e7b7c0b6c9f52eb/sweden-application-from-swedish-updated.pdf)
Visa Application Form (Chinese) (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/1kemK3ORA2RgkqGxkSXFgd/a8cbbfb33f361d1ad50f8c5d9ccce671/visa_application_form_chn_2024.pdf)
According to the Personal Information Protection Law of the People’s Republic of China and other applicable laws and regulations to protect your personal information, VFS Global must obtain the consent from you for the processing of your personal data and proceed with your visa application. Please download, print, fill, sign and submit the consent form along with your visa application form, without which we will not be able to proceed with the application lodgment. Click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/chn/en/swe/apply-visa)
To Download a Form:
- Click on the form link
- When the form opens, save the form on your computer.
OR
- Press the right button on your  mouse on the required link.
- Select Save  Target As from the list
- Save the form on your computer.

visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
For Chinese citizens who hold official passports (including service passports, public passports and diplomatic passports,) you should apply for Schengen visa via Ministry of Foreign Affairs
                                                                        (MFA) or Foreign Affairs Office (FAO).MFA or FAO should then deliver your visa application to Embassy of Sweden for processing.(Beijing Area)
For Chinese citizens who hold official passports (including service passports, public passports and diplomatic passports,) you should apply for Schengen visa via Foreign Affairs Office
                                                                        (FAO). 
Holders of official passports (including service passports, public passports and diplomatic passports) of nationalities other than China who apply visa for personal reasons with category
                                                                        like tourism and visiting family and friend should submit the Schengen visa applications to the Visa Application Centre. For the official purpose visa should still apply at Embassy
                                                                        of Sweden in Beijing or Consulate General of Sweden in Shanghai.

## Visa Fees
Visa Fees applicable for a Schengen visa are listed in the table below.

[Table:Tourism]

## Service Fees
Service Charge for Visa Applications: 18 EUR / 145 CNY per person (inclusive of Service Taxes). It is subject to change every month.

## Documents Required
- Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/chn/en/swe/travel-insurance)
In general the applicant is advised to provide  documentation in original. Additional documentation may be required. In case  the applicant cannot give the VAC the original version of the document, a copy  must be added and the original shown at the VAC. Without special instruction,  an English translation must be attached to any documents in Chinese. 
This category visa applicant is requested to contact the Embassy/Consulate General for case specific information. For further information please go through the Embassy of Sweden, Beijing (https://www.swedenabroad.se/en/embassies/china-beijing/)                                                                        or Consulate General of Sweden, Shanghai (https://www.swedenabroad.se/en/embassies/china-shanghai/) website.
To help us process your application quickly and accurately, please complete the Sweden Visa Application Form (VAF) electronically using the editable PDF available on this site, print the typed copy and carry it with appointment letter during your visit to the visa application centre. Completing the form electronically reduces counter waiting time and helps avoid errors caused by illegible handwriting.
Note: Applicants who arrive without a form completed electronically will still be accepted with a completed handwritten form; however, they should expect additional processing time at Visa Application Centre.

## Photo Specifications
A Photograph submitted with the application form should comply with the following specifications:

- Latest photograph not older than 6 months
- Colour photograph printed on normal photographic paper
- Should be passport sized 45mm x 35mm
- 70-90% of the photograph should be covered by face
- Full face straight profile photograph, 30-36 mm from point of the chin to head
- Should be taken against a light background
- Should be without sunglasses, hat or cap
- Photograph can be taken with headgear, after consideration on case to case basis if the applicant wears headgear on religious grounds.
- Spectacles should not have toned or reflecting glasses. The frame of the spectacles may not cover the eyes
- The mouth must be closed
Please note that photographs should meet the above mentioned specifications

## Processing Time
The processing time is counted from the date when the Embassy of Sweden in Beijing/Consulate General of Sweden in Shanghai receives the application documents; it will not include the
                                                                        delivery time between the Visa Application Center and Embassy/Consulate. Visa application submitted in Shanghai, Nanjing, Hangzhou Visa Application Center will be delivered to Consulate
                                                                        General of Sweden in Shanghai for processing. Visa application submitted in other Visa Application Centers will be delivered to the Embassy of Sweden in Beijing for processing. 
Usual application processing time is 15 calendar days from the date when the Embassy of Sweden in Beijing/Consulate General of Sweden in Shanghai receives the application documents, the
                                                                        processing time could up to 45 days in some cases. A visa application should be submitted at least 15 calendar days (excluding the day of documents' submission) before the intended
                                                                        visit and cannot be submitted earlier than six months before the intended date of arrival in the Schengen Area.
Please note that you may be required to appear before a Visa Officer for a personal interview at the Embassy/ Consulate General of Sweden in China prior to a decision being taken in your
                                                                        case. Embassy/Consulate General of Sweden also reserves the right to ask for additional supporting documents during the visa application process. Applicants are requested to read
                                                                        the information on this site carefully. The guidelines listed on this site helps you to prepare your documents as accurately as possible as possible which will reduce the chances
                                                                        of your application being incomplete or delayed.

Note: Applicants who arrive without a form completed electronically will still be accepted with a completed handwritten form; however, they should expect additional processing time at Visa Application Centre.

## Photo Specifications
A Photograph submitted with the application form should comply with the following specifications:

- Latest photograph not older than 6 months
- Colour photograph printed on normal photographic paper
- Should be passport sized 45mm x 35mm
- 70-90% of the photograph should be covered by face
- Full face straight profile photograph, 30-36 mm from point of the chin to head
- Should be taken against a light background
- Should be without sunglasses, hat or cap
- Photograph can be taken with headgear, after consideration on case to case basis if the applicant wears headgear on religious grounds.
- Spectacles should not have toned or reflecting glasses. The frame of the spectacles may not cover the eyes
- The mouth must be closed
Please note that photographs should meet the above mentioned specifications

visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
If you are planning to study more than 90 days in Sweden you need to apply for a residence permit. If you are planning to study less than 90 days you need to apply for a visa instead.

## Visa Fees
Visa Fees applicable for a Schengen visa are listed in the table below.

[Table:Tourism]

## Service Fees
Service Charge for Visa Applications: 18 EUR / 145 CNY per person (inclusive of Service Taxes). It is subject to change every month.

## Documents Required
- Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/chn/en/swe/travel-insurance)
In general the applicant is advised to provide  documentation in original. Additional documentation may be required. In case  the applicant cannot give the VAC the original version of the document, a copy  must be added and the original shown at the VAC. Without special instruction,  an English translation must be attached to any documents in Chinese. 
Checklist
Short Term Study (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2ZYFfTbSVsvzoBahXZuTks/aeb750778fedd689c7f43fa1487f247d/study-checklist.pdf)
To help us process your application quickly and accurately, please complete the Sweden Visa Application Form (VAF) electronically using the editable PDF available on this site, print the typed copy and carry it with appointment letter during your visit to the visa application centre. Completing the form electronically reduces counter waiting time and helps avoid errors caused by illegible handwriting.
Note: Applicants who arrive without a form completed electronically will still be accepted with a completed handwritten form; however, they should expect additional processing time at Visa Application Centre.

## Photo Specifications
A Photograph submitted with the application form should comply with the following specifications:

- Latest photograph not older than 6 months
- Colour photograph printed on normal photographic paper
- Should be passport sized 45mm x 35mm
- 70-90% of the photograph should be covered by face
- Full face straight profile photograph, 30-36 mm from point of the chin to head
- Should be taken against a light background
- Should be without sunglasses, hat or cap
- Photograph can be taken with headgear, after consideration on case to case basis if the applicant wears headgear on religious grounds.
- Spectacles should not have toned or reflecting glasses. The frame of the spectacles may not cover the eyes
- The mouth must be closed
Please note that photographs should meet the above mentioned specifications

## Processing Time
The processing time is counted from the date when the Embassy of Sweden in Beijing/Consulate General of Sweden in Shanghai receives the application documents; it will not include the
                                                                        delivery time between the Visa Application Center and Embassy/Consulate. Visa application submitted in Shanghai, Nanjing, Hangzhou Visa Application Center will be delivered to Consulate
                                                                        General of Sweden in Shanghai for processing. Visa application submitted in other Visa Application Centers will be delivered to the Embassy of Sweden in Beijing for processing. 
Usual application processing time is 15 calendar days from the date when the Embassy of Sweden in Beijing/Consulate General of Sweden in Shanghai receives the application documents, the
                                                                        processing time could up to 45 days in some cases. A visa application should be submitted at least 15 calendar days (excluding the day of documents' submission) before the intended
                                                                        visit and cannot be submitted earlier than six months before the intended date of arrival in the Schengen Area.
Please note that you may be required to appear before a Visa Officer for a personal interview at the Embassy/ Consulate General of Sweden in China prior to a decision being taken in your
                                                                        case. Embassy/Consulate General of Sweden also reserves the right to ask for additional supporting documents during the visa application process. Applicants are requested to read
                                                                        the information on this site carefully. The guidelines listed on this site helps you to prepare your documents as accurately as possible as possible which will reduce the chances

visa_types.overview:
Overview
An ADS visa issued by a Schengen country permits travel within the Schengen area in accordance with a pre-determined itinerary. ADS visas are issued to members of tourists groups of at
                                                                        least 5 persons. The maximum duration of stay is 30 days.
ADS visas are applied for via an ADS-accredited travel agency. Applications are submitted directly (i.e. not by post) by the travel agency’s designated couriers.

To help us process your application quickly and accurately, please complete the Sweden Visa Application Form (VAF) electronically using the editable PDF available on this site, print the typed copy and carry it with appointment letter during your visit to the visa application centre. Completing the form electronically reduces counter waiting time and helps avoid errors caused by illegible handwriting.
Note: Applicants who arrive without a form completed electronically will still be accepted with a completed handwritten form; however, they should expect additional processing time at Visa Application Centre.

## Photo Specifications
A Photograph submitted with the application form should comply with the following specifications:

- Latest photograph not older than 6 months
- Colour photograph printed on normal photographic paper
- Should be passport sized 45mm x 35mm
- 70-90% of the photograph should be covered by face
- Full face straight profile photograph, 30-36 mm from point of the chin to head
- Should be taken against a light background
- Should be without sunglasses, hat or cap
- Photograph can be taken with headgear, after consideration on case to case basis if the applicant wears headgear on religious grounds.
- Spectacles should not have toned or reflecting glasses. The frame of the spectacles may not cover the eyes
- The mouth must be closed
Please note that photographs should meet the above mentioned specifications

## Processing Time
The processing time is counted from the date when the Embassy of Sweden in Beijing/Consulate General of Sweden in Shanghai receives the application documents; it will not include the
                                                                        delivery time between the Visa Application Center and Embassy/Consulate. Visa application submitted in Shanghai, Nanjing, Hangzhou Visa Application Center will be delivered to Consulate
                                                                        General of Sweden in Shanghai for processing. Visa application submitted in other Visa Application Centers will be delivered to the Embassy of Sweden in Beijing for processing. 
Usual application processing time is 15 calendar days from the date when the Embassy of Sweden in Beijing/Consulate General of Sweden in Shanghai receives the application documents, the
                                                                        processing time could up to 45 days in some cases. A visa application should be submitted at least 15 calendar days (excluding the day of documents' submission) before the intended
                                                                        visit and cannot be submitted earlier than six months before the intended date of arrival in the Schengen Area.
Please note that you may be required to appear before a Visa Officer for a personal interview at the Embassy/ Consulate General of Sweden in China prior to a decision being taken in your
                                                                        case. Embassy/Consulate General of Sweden also reserves the right to ask for additional supporting documents during the visa application process. Applicants are requested to read
                                                                        the information on this site carefully. The guidelines listed on this site helps you to prepare your documents as accurately as possible as possible which will reduce the chances
                                                                        of your application being incomplete or delayed.
